items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` **Service directory:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` **Triggered by:** PR #57982 by `@ArthurMa1978` ## What Changed The 1.4.0 GA release was published on 2026-04-09. Changes include: - Upgraded API version to **2025-01-01** - All classes and methods dependent on the retiring **2014-04-01** API version were marked `[Obsolete]` (from beta.2) - AOT compatibility was enabled (from beta.2) - Upgraded dependent `Azure.Core` to 1.52.0 and `Azure.ResourceManager` to 1.14.0 ## Gaps Found - **No SQL-specific Key Concepts content** — the `## Key concepts` section only has a generic link to Azure SDK guidelines with no explanation of SQL resource types, hierarchy (`SqlServerResource` → `SqlDatabaseResource`), or management patterns - **No SQL-specific Examples with code snippets** — the `## Examples` section only links to a generic samples page; the README has no inline code examples showing common operations (create server, create database, list elastic pools, etc.) - **No migration guidance for deprecated 2014-04-01 API methods** — the 1.4.0 release marks many methods as obsolete (retiring 2014-04-01 API), but the README and Troubleshooting section offer no guidance for users who need to migrate away from them - **README missing the new API version capabilities** — the README does not mention the newly supported `2025-01-01` API version or what features it brings - **`## Documentation` section is non-standard** — the README includes a separate `## Documentation` section that does not appear in the canonical template (see `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md`); its content should be folded into `## Key concepts`
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` — **edit** Replace the current minimal `## Key concepts` section with substantive content: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.Sql` library provides access to the Azure SQL resource hierarchy. The main entry points are: - **`SqlServerResource`** — represents an Azure SQL logical server. Retrieved via `resourceGroup.GetSqlServers()` or `resourceGroup.GetSqlServer(serverName)`. - **`SqlDatabaseResource`** — represents a database inside a logical server. Retrieved via `server.GetSqlDatabases()` or `server.GetSqlDatabase(databaseName)`. - **`SqlElasticPoolResource`** — represents an elastic pool for sharing compute resources across multiple databases. - **`ManagedInstanceResource`** — represents an Azure SQL Managed Instance. - **`ManagedDatabaseResource`** — represents a database inside a Managed Instance. All long-running operations (Create, Update, Delete) return an `ArmOperation` or `ArmOperation` and accept a `WaitUntil` parameter to control whether the call blocks until completion. > **Note:** Methods that target the retired 2014-04-01 REST API version are marked `[Obsolete]` as of version 1.4.0. Refer to the [SQL REST API retirement notice]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) for details and migration guidance. ``` Remove the separate `## Documentation` section and incorporate its quickstart/API-reference links into the `## Key concepts` section or `## Next steps`. Replace the current `## Examples` section content with a snippet placeholder and brief prose: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a SQL server and database ```C# Snippet:Sql_CreateServerAndDatabase ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **Test file to create:** `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/tests/Samples/Sample3_CreateServerAndDatabase.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using Azure.ResourceManager.Sql; using Azure.ResourceManager.Sql.Models; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Sql.Tests.Samples { public class Sample3_CreateServerAndDatabase : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] public async Task CreateServerAndDatabaseAsync() { #region Snippet:Sql_CreateServerAndDatabase ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups().GetAsync("myResourceGroup"); // Create a SQL server SqlServerData serverData = new SqlServerData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")) { AdministratorLogin = "myAdminUser", AdministratorLoginPassword = "myAdminPassword123!", }; ArmOperation serverOp = await resourceGroup.GetSqlServers() .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "myServer", serverData); SqlServerResource server = serverOp.Value; // Create a database on the server SqlDatabaseData databaseData = new SqlDatabaseData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")); ArmOperation dbOp = await server.GetSqlDatabases() .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "myDatabase", databaseData); SqlDatabaseResource database = dbOp.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created database: {database.Data.Name}"); #endregion #if !SNIPPET // Cleanup await database.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed); await server.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed); #endif } } } ``` - **Snippet name:** `Sql_CreateServerAndDatabase` - **README location:** Inside the new `## Examples` section, under the `### Create a SQL server and database` heading ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/README.md` must contain these sections. Current status: | Section | Status | Action needed | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Replace with SQL-specific content (see Step 1) | | Examples | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add inline snippet placeholder (see Step 1) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | Add note about deprecated 2014-04-01 API methods | | Next steps | ✅ Present | None | | Contributing | ✅ Present | None | | Documentation *(non-standard)* | ❌ Remove | Merge links into Key concepts / Next steps | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 sqlmanagement` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 sqlmanagement` 5. `dotnet format sdk/sqlmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.Sql/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [SQL] Docs: Update README for 1.4.0 release — key concepts, examples, and deprecated API guidance
